prompt: Top-down flat-lay over a worn plywood workbench. Center frame: a contractor's well-used plastic license card showing visible edge wear and a faded laminated photo area, but the entire surface text and photo are intentionally indistinct, no legible letters or numbers. To its right: a torn-open white government-style mailing envelope with a subtle return-address block (also non-readable) and a folded letter peeking out. To the left: a brass house key on a leather fob, a stub of carpenter pencil. Loose sawdust scattered on the bench. Shot with a 50mm normal lens directly overhead, dusk warm tungsten light from a single hanging shop bulb just out of frame, warm honey tones with deep shadow falloff at the corners. Slightly desaturated palette, a red mechanical pencil clip in the corner is the only saturated color. NOT HGTV bright. Documentary, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text anywhere.
